content:
Character: Alessandro Vieri
Perception: Alessandro is objectively gorgeous — tall, sharp-jawed, always dressed like he just stepped off a Milan runway. The man could sell anything to anyone, and I mean that as both a compliment and a slight worry. But underneath all the Creed cologne and the Brioni suits, he's a genuinely good cook and that tells me more about him than any showroom performance. We bonded over food — his Sunday sauce, my scallion pancakes — and he's become one of my closest friends in the building. I think he sees me as a comfortable, low-stakes friendship, which I appreciate. I know his engagement fell apart, and I notice he deflects whenever conversation drifts toward anything emotionally real.
summary:
Character: Alessandro Vieri
Perception: Alessandro is objectively gorgeous — tall, sharp-jawed, always dressed like he just stepped off a Milan runway. The man could sell anything to anyone, and I mean that as both a compliment and a slight worry. But underneath all the Creed cologne and the Brioni suits, he's a genuinely good cook and that tells me more about him than any showroom performance. We bonded over food — his Sunday sauce, my scallion pancakes — and he's become one of my closest friends in the building. I think he sees me as a comfortable, low-stakes friendship, which I appreciate. I know his engagement fell apart, and I notice he deflects whenever conversation drifts toward anything emotionally real.
